title: French A1–A2 + C1–C2 taxonomy backbone
note: Lightweight CEFR level + grammar-topic reference used to tag generated/imported French content. Covers A1 and A2, plus an advanced C1–C2 track (expression, argumentation, politics, workplace, complex ideas). B1–B2 are declared as levels but not yet populated with topics.

levels.title: Foundations
levels.descriptor: Understands and uses familiar everyday expressions and very basic phrases.

levels.title: Everyday Fluency
levels.descriptor: Communicates in simple routine tasks on familiar matters.

levels.title: Independent User
levels.descriptor: Deals with most situations while travelling; describes experiences, opinions, plans.

levels.title: Upper-Intermediate
levels.descriptor: Interacts with fluency and spontaneity; produces clear, detailed text on many subjects.

levels.title: Autonomous — Advanced Expression
levels.descriptor: Expresses ideas fluently and spontaneously; uses language flexibly for social, academic and professional purposes; produces clear, well-structured, detailed text on complex subjects.

levels.title: Mastery — Expert Discourse
levels.descriptor: Understands virtually everything with ease; expresses spontaneously, very fluently and precisely, differentiating finer shades of meaning even in the most complex situations.

grammarTopics.title: être & avoir
grammarTopics.description: The two core verbs 'to be' (être) and 'to have' (avoir).

grammarTopics.title: Subject pronouns
grammarTopics.description: je, tu, il/elle/on, nous, vous, ils/elles.

grammarTopics.title: Present tense (regular -er verbs)
grammarTopics.description: Regular present-tense conjugation of -er verbs (parler, habiter).

grammarTopics.title: Questions & question words
grammarTopics.description: est-ce que, qu'est-ce que, où, quand, comment, pourquoi; yes/no questions and intonation.

grammarTopics.title: Articles & noun gender
grammarTopics.description: Definite le/la/les and indefinite un/une/des; noun gender.

grammarTopics.title: Present tense (-ir/-re verbs)
grammarTopics.description: Regular -ir (finir) and -re (vendre) present conjugation.

grammarTopics.title: Common irregular verbs
grammarTopics.description: High-frequency irregulars: aller, faire, venir, pouvoir, vouloir.

grammarTopics.title: Plurals & numbers
grammarTopics.description: Plural formation (-s/-x) and cardinal numbers.

grammarTopics.title: Negation (ne … pas)
grammarTopics.description: Basic negation with ne … pas / jamais / rien.

grammarTopics.title: Possessive adjectives
grammarTopics.description: mon/ma/mes, ton/ta/tes, son/sa/ses, notre, votre, leur; agreement.

grammarTopics.title: Adjective agreement & position
grammarTopics.description: Adjectives agree in gender and number; common position rules.

grammarTopics.title: il y a (there is / there are)
grammarTopics.description: Impersonal 'il y a' to state existence and quantities.

grammarTopics.title: Basic prepositions
grammarTopics.description: à, de, en, chez, avec; à + le = au, de + le = du.

grammarTopics.title: aller + infinitive (near future)
grammarTopics.description: Talking about plans with the futur proche.

grammarTopics.title: Passé composé
grammarTopics.description: Compound past with avoir/être + past participle; agreement with être.

grammarTopics.title: Imparfait
grammarTopics.description: Past habits, descriptions and background; regular and irregular forms.

grammarTopics.title: Pronominal (reflexive) verbs
grammarTopics.description: Reflexive pronouns (me, te, se…) and daily-routine verbs.

grammarTopics.title: Partitive articles
grammarTopics.description: du, de la, de l', des; de after negation and quantities.

grammarTopics.title: Futur simple
grammarTopics.description: Simple future; regular stems and key irregular stems (être, avoir, aller, faire).

grammarTopics.title: Comparative & superlative
grammarTopics.description: plus/moins/aussi … que; le plus/le moins; meilleur and mieux.

grammarTopics.title: Object pronouns (COD/COI)
grammarTopics.description: Direct (le/la/les) and indirect (lui/leur) object pronouns.

grammarTopics.title: Prepositions with places & countries
grammarTopics.description: à/en/au/aux with cities and countries; venir de.

grammarTopics.title: Le subjonctif présent
grammarTopics.description: Present subjunctive after expressions of doubt, emotion, will and necessity (il faut que, vouloir que, bien que).

grammarTopics.title: Subjonctif ou indicatif
grammarTopics.description: Choosing mood: certainty/opinion (indicatif) vs doubt/subjectivity (subjonctif); penser que vs ne pas penser que.

grammarTopics.title: Les registres de langue
grammarTopics.description: Soutenu / courant / familier: lexical, syntactic and stylistic markers and when to shift register.

grammarTopics.title: La mise en relief
grammarTopics.description: Emphasis and cleft structures: c'est … qui/que, ce qui/ce que … c'est, dislocation and focus.

grammarTopics.title: Les connecteurs logiques
grammarTopics.description: Articulating discourse: addition, opposition, cause, consequence, illustration, conclusion (par ailleurs, en revanche, par conséquent).

grammarTopics.title: Concession et opposition
grammarTopics.description: bien que/quoique (+ subjonctif), malgré, avoir beau, tandis que, or; conceding and contrasting.

grammarTopics.title: Cause et conséquence
grammarTopics.description: étant donné que, du fait que, si bien que, d'où, au point que; expressing reason and result.

grammarTopics.title: L'hypothèse et la condition
grammarTopics.description: si-clauses (three systems), conditionnel présent/passé, à condition que, au cas où.

grammarTopics.title: Le discours indirect
grammarTopics.description: Reported speech and the concordance des temps; shifting tense, pronouns and time markers.

grammarTopics.title: Politesse et atténuation
grammarTopics.description: Conditional for diplomacy and hedging (je voudrais, il faudrait, on pourrait envisager).

grammarTopics.title: La voix passive et ses alternatives
grammarTopics.description: Passive with être + participe; on, se faire + infinitif and pronominal passive as alternatives.

grammarTopics.title: L'entretien d'embauche
grammarTopics.description: Presenting skills and experience: arguing strengths, answering competency questions, professional self-presentation.

grammarTopics.title: Les pronoms relatifs complexes
grammarTopics.description: dont, lequel/duquel/auquel, ce qui/ce que/ce dont, où; combining clauses precisely.

grammarTopics.title: Le gérondif et le participe présent
grammarTopics.description: en + participe (simultaneity/manner/condition) vs participe présent clause; contrast with the adjectif verbal.

grammarTopics.title: La nominalisation
grammarTopics.description: Condensing information into noun phrases; deverbal nouns and headline/report style.

grammarTopics.title: L'écrit professionnel
grammarTopics.description: Emails, reports and cover letters: structure, formules de politesse, cohesion and appropriate register.

grammarTopics.title: Le discours politique
grammarTopics.description: Analysing and producing persuasive political discourse: ethos/pathos/logos, slogans, rhetorical questions.

grammarTopics.title: Les figures de style
grammarTopics.description: Rhetorical devices: anaphore, gradation, antithèse, métaphore, chiasme for persuasive effect.

grammarTopics.title: L'implicite et la nuance
grammarTopics.description: Litote, euphémisme, ironie and sous-entendu; conveying meaning indirectly.

grammarTopics.title: Le subjonctif passé
grammarTopics.description: Past subjunctive for anteriority in subjective and concessive clauses; nuanced modality.

grammarTopics.title: La cohésion textuelle
grammarTopics.description: Reference and progression: anaphore/cataphore, reprises pronominales/lexicales, thème–rhème, expert connectors.

grammarTopics.title: La précision lexicale
grammarTopics.description: Collocations, near-synonyms and terminologie; choosing the exact word and avoiding répétition.

grammarTopics.title: Expliquer et vulgariser
grammarTopics.description: Making complex systems clear: definition, reformulation (c'est-à-dire), analogy, exemplification.

grammarTopics.title: Les structures impersonnelles
grammarTopics.description: Objectivation: il s'avère que, il en résulte que, il convient de, on constate que.

grammarTopics.title: L'essai argumentatif
grammarTopics.description: Dissertation structure: problématique, thèse–antithèse–synthèse, plan and transitions.

grammarTopics.title: Concession et réfutation
grammarTopics.description: certes … mais, quand bien même, quitte à, n'en … pas moins; conceding then refuting.

grammarTopics.title: L'hypothèse et l'irréel
grammarTopics.description: Plus-que-parfait du subjonctif and conditionnel passé 2e forme; literary counterfactuals.

grammarTopics.title: La modalisation
grammarTopics.description: Marking stance and certainty: modal verbs/adverbs, subjectivity vs objectivity, prise de position.

grammarTopics.title: Le passé simple
grammarTopics.description: Literary narrative past (passé simple and passé antérieur): recognition and use in formal writing.

grammarTopics.title: La proposition participiale
grammarTopics.description: Participial and absolute constructions (le travail terminé, …); condensing subordinate clauses.

grammarTopics.title: L'inversion stylistique
grammarTopics.description: Stylistic subject–verb inversion after adverbs and in soutenu syntax (peut-être, ainsi, aussi).

grammarTopics.title: Locutions et idiomes
grammarTopics.description: Idioms, set phrases and learned expressions; figurative and cultural fluency.
